target: eval_polygamma (Scalar n, Tensor x) — polygamma_n(x) over a tensor. Serial + Literal-backed. polygamma_approx is a heavy series/asymptotic evaluation per element (the most compute-heavy unary special function).
lever: Thread the dense-F64 x case: read as_f64_slice and apply polygamma_approx(n, x) across scoped threads into a dense f64 output (>= 1<<13). For F64Bits, polygamma_literal_to_f64 == from_bits == the slice value, so bit-identical to the serial loop. Non-dense / non-F64 keep serial.
isomorphism: BIT-EXACT. threaded_dense_polygamma_bit_identical_to_reference (n=0,1,2,3 over a dense tensor == element-wise polygamma_approx(n,x), bit-for-bit). 1111 fj-lax tests pass (remote-verified).
decision: SHIP. 20.5x — polygamma_approx is so compute-heavy that threading across cores (plus dense extract) gives near-linear scaling. Bit-exact, zero-regression. Committed git-first (no rch between edit and commit) per the rch-source-clobber workaround.
